entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop Sorghum bicolor used in breeding programs

entities.name: GRAIN MOLD
entities.description: A fungal disease affecting the grains of sorghum

entities.name: GRAIN MOLD RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to resist infection by grain mold

entities.name: B-LINES
entities.description: Advanced breeding lines used as female parents in hybrid seed production

entities.name: PEDIGREE BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding method involving selection of progenies based on ancestry

entities.name: SINGLE- AND THREE-WAY CROSSES
entities.description: Specific crossing strategies used to combine traits in breeding

entities.name: FIELD SCREENINGS
entities.description: The process of evaluating plant lines under natural disease pressure in the field

entities.name: SGMRSN
entities.description: Sorghum Grain Mold Resistance Stability Nursery, a collaborative testing framework

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the resistance stability trials were conducted

entities.name: HIGH YIELD
entities.description: The capacity of a crop to produce a large amount of grain

entities.name: RESISTANCE STABILITY
entities.description: The consistency of disease resistance across diverse geographical locations

relationships.description: Sorghum is susceptible to grain mold
Sorghum is bred for grain mold resistance
B-lines are developed to possess grain mold resistance
Pedigree breeding is used to develop B-lines
Crosses are part of the pedigree breeding process
Field screenings are used to select resistant B-lines
SGMRSN is used to test resistance stability
SGMRSN tests the stability of elite B-lines
SGMRSN trials were conducted at diverse locations in India
B-lines are evaluated for resistance stability
